title: Congress appeals to Wangchuk to end fast, says ‘his concerns are ours and that of other Oppn. parties’
content: K.C. Venugopal, in a post on X, assured that the party will continue to “confront the Modi government and demand the Education Minister’s resignation”

title: Why run clubs and trekking groups are becoming friendship hubs for adults in Thiruvananthapuram
content: From runners to trekkers, outdoor communities in Kerala’s capital are helping residents build friendships, stay accountable and improve their physical and mental well-being

title: COVID-19 case at AIIMS Mangalagiri; Guntur health authorities step up precautions
content: DMHO K. Vijayalakshmi said the district administration is taking all preventive and containment measures; positive samples are being sent to NIV, Pune, for genome sequencing

title: Navi Mumbai, Guwahati airports among world’s ‘most beautiful’
content: Adani Airport Holdings Limited says Prix Versailles, one of the leading architecture and design awards, recognised the two airports

title: Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant data leak: What happened and what we know | Explained
content: Files linked to Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant surfaced on the Dark Web after a reported ransomware incident. This explainer breaks down what was leaked, what it means, and what remains protected

title: Mallikarjun Kharge writes to PM Modi, seeks all-party meeting on delimitation
content: Mallikarjun Kharge requested Prime Minister Narendra Modi to provide the Opposition adequate time to study the proposals on delimitation before it is introduced in Parliament

title: IAS officers’ reshuffle in Tamil Nadu: J. Radhakrishnan, Supriya Sahu transferred
content: P. Umanath, formerly Chairman and Managing Director, Tamil Nadu Civil Supplies Corporation, is posted as Industries Commissioner and Director of Industries and Commerce, replacing Nirmal Raj

title: Nurses in Kerala’s Kannur call off their 62-day strike as Koyili Hospital signs settlement
content: Nurses of six private hospitals in Kannur district were on strike over wage revision and service condition issues

title: TMC MP Koel Mallick resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: “I do hereby resign from the membership of Rajya Sabha, which may please be accepted with immediate effect,” Ms. Mallick said in her resignation letter.

title: Police conduct Operation Night Safety across Anantapur district
content: The Police also carried out ‘Face Wash and Go’ programme at checkposts to help drivers on the highways overcome drowsiness

title: PM Modi to flag off India’s first hydrogen-powered train in Haryana
content: Unlike traditional trains, it generates its own electricity onboard through a chemical reaction between hydrogen and atmospheric oxygen, leaving behind water vapour; Mr. Modi will also inaugurate or lay the foundation for infrastructure worth over ₹25,000 crore during the day-long visit to Haryana and Punjab tomorrow

title: Maharashtra’s Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana | Explained
content: The number of beneficiaries under Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana has fallen from 2.43 crore to 1.66 crore. We explain why, and what the CAG found in its audit

title: Third language must stop, not start, in Class 9: Justice Nagarathna
content: Court advises new TVK-led State government to not deny the children of Tamil Nadu the benefit of Central schools through the Navodaya scheme, suggests the State could have its ‘own education system’ without denying children the option of enrolling in Navodaya schools

title: Bunty Yadav kidnapping-murder case: Prime accused injured in police encounter
content: The accused, identified as Ravish, had been absconding since the alleged abduction of Bunty Yadav on July 6

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Bidadi project: Hours after Karnataka CM announces review panel, government issues final land notification for acquiring 4,944.49 acres across four villages
content: This is the biggest tranche of land notified yet. The first set pertained to only 518.45 acres in three villages

title: Six persons arrested for opening mule accounts in Chikkamagaluru
content: According to a complaint, some persons were persuaded by the accused to open accounts in IndusInd Bank with the promise of financial benefits

title: Andhra Pradesh’s retail inflation above national average for fifth month running
content: The State’s retail inflation rose to 4.9% in May 2026 against a national 3.9%, with personal care and miscellaneous services seeing the steepest rise; the government has placed inflation on its ‘Needs Attention’ watchlist

title: BJP leader Vanathi Srinivasan urges Tamil Nadu CM to ensure receipt of Cauvery water from Karnataka
content: Referring to reports that Karnataka has declined to share water from Cauvery due to Tamil Nadu, Ms. Srinivasan contended that it was an indication that the rights of Tamil Nadu was being undermined gradually.

title: More rain in store for Odisha as low-pressure over Bay of Bengal to intensify: IMD
content: Weather system likely to intensify into a well-marked low-pressure area and move northwestward across north Odisha and Gangetic West Bengal during the next 12 hours says, IMD bulletin

title: Assam’s out-of-school children dream of becoming MLAs, teachers
content: Some of the 45 such children who attended three 10-day motivational camps want to become teachers to impact lives around them

title: Relief for bulk waste generators as Minister promises to end vendor monopoly in Bengaluru
content: Currently, BSWML has empanelled five companies, one for each corporation, to collect waste from bulk generators, which has led to a monopoly

title: Sonam Wangchuk loses over 9 kg, weakens significantly on 19th day of hunger strike: CJP
content: Sonam Wangchuk remains under constant medical supervision, the CJP said in a statement, adding that he is still ‘mentally alert’, even as he has weakened significantly

title: Hakki-Pikki woman accused of ‘selling daughter’ in Senegal
content: The girl has since been rescued by the local police, and efforts were on to bring her back to India, according to Karnataka Hakki-Pikki Budakattu Sanghatane

title: India-U.K. trade deal explained | What gets cheaper, who stands to gain?
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides. Here’s the complete picture

title: Government officials facing corruption charges not entitled to promotion: Madras High Court
content: Division Bench sets aside a single judge’s order to grant promotion to a Deputy Inspector General of Registration who ended up facing corruption charges after the inclusion of his name in the promotion panel

title: Centre floats another draft of CAFE-III, gives special recognition to ethanol and biofuel cars
content: Industry stakeholders have until August 6 to respond to the latest draft; the proposed CAFE norms have sharply divided the automobile industry, with manufacturers of smaller and larger vehicles taking opposing positions and competing proposals emerging over how the regulations should be framed

title: Watch: Why is the Punjab Congress in a mess ahead of next year’s elections? | Talking Politics
content: With less than a year to go for the 2027 Punjab Assembly election, the Congress finds itself grappling with another leadership tussle. Former Chief Minister Charanjit Singh Channi’s supporters have openly demanded that he replace Amrinder Singh Raja Warring as president of the Punjab Pradesh Congress Committee (PPCC), exposing the factional fault lines within the State unit. This brings back memories of the run-up to the 2022 Assembly elections, when months of confrontation between the then CM Amarinder Singh and Navjot Sidhu led to the party’s defeat. Is history repeating itself?

title: After decade-long demand, Karnataka government presents draft Apartment bill
content: The major overhaul has been proposed in terms of fixing the competent authority, the safety of older apartment complexes, dispute resolution, and accountability

title: What is 100% ethanol blending? | Explained
content: Increasing Sustainable Aviation Fuel production without further stressing sugarcane production will depend on how quickly second-generation ethanol can be produced in commercial volumes

title: Can’t English be considered an indigenous Indian language, asks Supreme Court
content: SC questions the use of the colonially loaded term ‘native’ in CBSE circular, says the push for three-language scheme may be in the spirit of the Constitutional goal to use Indian languages for official purposes

title: CBI arrests MD of Bengaluru-based Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited for taking bribe of ₹5 lakh
content: Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited (KAPL) Managing Director Anurag Danayak was allegedly caught red-handed taking the bribe from a company that is a service agent of KAPL, according to a CBI spokesperson

title: Delhi HC directs daily medical monitoring of Sonam Wangchuk amid indefinite hunger strike
content: The petition argued that the government could not remain a passive spectator, contending that failure to intervene could amount to ‘abetment of suicide’ under Section 108 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023

title: Bombay High Court seeks Centre’s response on comedian Kunal Kamra's plea against ‘Sahyog Portal’
content: Kunal Kamra had claimed that the Sahyog Portal was a tool used by the government for online censorship and the amended rules enabled the government to order the takedown of content without adequate safeguards

title: Six men arrested for stealing silver-laced soil from Hindustan Photo Films factory in Nilgiris
content: It is believed that sludge from producing the films would have trace amounts of gold and silver, which would then accrue in the soil in certain parts of the factory where they were stored. One of the arrested men is Suresh, the branch secretary of the TVK from Ward 2 in Udhagamandalam.

title: May rethink over delimitation Bill if Opposition's amendments incorporated: Sanjay Raut
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut, talking to reporters ahead of the Ram Raksha Andolan on July 18, says he will rethink delimation Bill if Oppostion’s demand for the Bill are met amid ongoing protests

title: Andhra Pradesh signs MoU with UN-Habitat for Inclusive urban development
content: The partnership aims to establish a Centre of Excellence for Urban Affairs in Amaravati and focus on alleviating urban poverty, improving livelihood of urban poor, and promoting inclusive city development

title: Free Trade Agreements, including the one with India and UK, offer favourable market access to MSMEs: FKCCI
content: For MSMEs, which number over 6.3 crore across India and contribute nearly 30% to the GDP, the effective use of FTAs can be truly transformational, said FKCCI President Uma Reddy.

title: Mass fish kill: Two years on, Periyar fishers await compensation
content: Fishermen have urged the government to consider extending due compensation to the fisherfolk as their daily earnings were hit following the contamination of the river

title: CM V.D. Satheesan launches Thiruvonam Bumper Lottery with ₹30 crore first prize
content: Tickets to be sold from July 20, draw on September 26; the ticket price of the lottery remains unchanged at ₹500.

title: Monsoon season power crisis: Power curbs likely to continue in Kerala
content: Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph said that the State is currently facing a 500 MW to 600 MW deficit in availability

title: A new era of Indian ecology looks to its horizons, and to the ground
content: The second Indian Wildlife Ecology Conference at Ashoka University was a forum where researchers from universities, government agencies, NGOs, and field stations together figured out where the discipline is heading — using insights into evolutionary history, long-term monitoring, public policy, technology, and public health

title: Why are rival Trinamool Congress factions competing to hold the July 21 Martyrs’ Day Rally? | Explained
content: For more than three decades, the Martyrs’ Day rally in West Bengal has been synonymous with Mamata Banerjee. This year, rival Trinamool factions and the Congress are competing to claim its political legacy

title: Congress leaders discuss party strategy for upcoming Parliament session
content: Senior Congress leaders, including party chief Mallikarjun Kharge, Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi and other senior leaders deliberated on key issues to be taken up during the session.

title: An anthem for a vanishing craft: Thazhappattu makes it to school curriculum
content: Screwpine art, which involves transforming simple leaves into green, sustainable thazhappas (mats) — a craft that was once a thriving cottage industry in Thazhava but has slowly declined due to modern challenges – receives a significant tribute in the form of Thazhappattu, a song written by Ani Mank.

title: Raut urges Fadnavis to take part in 'Ram Raksha Andolan' of Sena (UBT) in Nagpur
content: Sena (UBT) head and former State CM Uddhav Thackeray had earlier said that he would take part in the Ram Raksha Andolan organised by the party in Nagpur, the hometown of CM Fadnavis, on Saturday (July 16).

title: Beyond momo: Meghalaya’s chefs are reclaiming the state’s culinary identity
content: In Shillong, chefs behind A’Origins, Rynsan and Lady Aiko are reclaiming Meghalaya’s culinary heritage with dishes built around fermented bamboo shoots, smoked meats, forest produce and traditional cooking techniques.

title: 12-year-old Bengaluru boy dies after collapsing in school; family gets CCTV camera footage one month later, alleges negligence
content: The CCTV camera footage reportedly shows the boy informing his teacher that he was feeling unwell before returning to the classroom. Moments later, he allegedly collapsed on his bench

title: Hurdles before oil exploration on Assam-Nagaland boundary | Explained
content: Extremist and traditional tribe-based organisations in Nagaland do not want oil majors to resume oil exploration and extraction until the inter-State border dispute and the ‘Indo-Naga political issue’ are resolved

title: 'Manmohan govt engaged with Anna Hazare ... ': Omar says Centre unmoved by Wangchuk
content: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah supports Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk is protesting alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, demanding accountability. His health has significantly deteriorated after nineteen days of fasting. Doctors warn of serious health complications if the hunger strike continues. The Delhi High Court has ordered daily medical examinations for Wangchuk.

title: Canada to stop accepting new Parents and Grandparents Program applications
content: Canada is pausing new applications for the Parents and Grandparents Program. Existing applications will be processed, and 15,000 people will be approved in 2026. Family reunification remains a priority, and the super visa offers extended visits for relatives. The government aims to reduce processing times and improve predictability for families. Canada also warns citizens about potential marriage fraud in immigration sponsorship cases.

title: China charms world, now more popular than US; Xi tops Trump on trust: Report
content: Recent global surveys reveal that China's image is increasingly favorable compared to that of the United States. Under Xi Jinping's leadership, he commands greater trust internationally than Donald Trump does. While personal freedoms remain a strength for the US, this advantage is slowly diminishing as China is perceived to engage less in interventionist policies worldwide. These trends underscore a noteworthy shift in international perceptions.

title: 'Lesson for playing with his life': How secret marriage led to Bengaluru student's murder
content: Two brothers allegedly threatened a woman before she was fatally stabbed at her Bengaluru home.  The victim, Amrutha, died from her injuries two days after the brutal attack occurred.  Police arrested brothers Dhanush and Surya in connection with the violent murder case.  Amrutha ended her relationship with Dhanush after discovering his prior marriage and child.  The dispute between the families reportedly escalated after the breakup, leading to this tragedy.

title: Falkland poster row spills over: British govt seeks FIFA action against Argentina
content: Argentina defeated England 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup semifinal on Wednesday in Atlanta. During the post-match celebrations, Argentine players posed with a banner handed to them by supporters in the stands that read, "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" ("The Malvinas are Argentine"). Argentina refers to the Falkland Islands as Islas Malvinas.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for Watts Towers Arts Center Campus improvements. This investment will expand usable park space and introduce new cooling features. The project aims to strengthen the campus as a cultural hub before the 2028 Olympics. Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass welcomed the significant milestone for the community. This funding advances commitments to green space and climate resilience efforts.

title: Marriage certified by a notary? MP HC says they have no such power
content: The Madhya Pradesh high court suspended a notary's license for preparing a fake marriage document. The court ruled that notaries lack authority to function as marriage officers. A young woman was freed after alleging coercion into a sham court marriage. She expressed her desire to return to her parents and continue studies. The court ordered further investigation into the notary's actions.

title: California may get $65M to leave 65 billion gallons in Lake Mead
content: Historically low water levels are plaguing Lake Mead, affecting millions in various states and Mexico. To combat this, federal funds will support efforts to keep water in the reservoir this year, coupled with initiatives aimed at conserving agricultural water annually. While these measures are crucial for now, achieving lasting change will necessitate cooperation among Colorado River Basin states regarding future water management.

title: Ancient sea once split North America, its creatures still shine like gems
content: Millions of years ago, South Dakota was covered by a vast inland sea. This ancient seaway supported diverse marine life, including ammonites and marine reptiles. Today, fossils from this period are displayed at the Smithsonian's National Museum of Natural History. These ancient shells still shine due to their mother-of-pearl composition. Discoveries continue to reveal insights into prehistoric marine ecosystems and Earth's history.

title: Google founder’s net worth crossed $300bn as Warren Buffet admits mistake
content: Larry Page's net worth exceeded three hundred billion dollars after Alphabet's stock climbed.  This significant rise added billions to the co-founder's considerable personal wealth.  Warren Buffett recently admitted he regretted not investing in Alphabet sooner.  Berkshire Hathaway initiated its first investment in Alphabet last year.  That initial investment has since grown substantially in value.

title: Renting a home without a written agreement? Here's why it may be risky
content: In India, verbal rental agreements often lead to misunderstandings, as they lack essential written terms and legal safeguards. This scenario exposes tenants to unpredictable rent increases and abrupt evictions. The absence of documentation complicates security deposits, and property repairs frequently result in disputes. To protect both parties, written contracts are now a requirement under the Model Tenancy Act in states that have adopted it.

title: 'Convene an all-party meeting': Kharge writes to PM Modi on revised Delimitation Bill
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to call an all-party meeting. He requested adequate time for opposition parties to study the revised delimitation proposals. The Congress party plans to strongly oppose the proposed delimitation bill if reintroduced. They will also work to maintain unity among all opposition parties. The party supports women's reservation but opposes linking it to delimitation.

title: 'Wanted papers': Drunk son kills father over Rs 150cr property dispute in Ghaziabad
content: A 32-year-old man allegedly shot his father dead after a heated argument. The dispute began when the father confronted his son for returning home drunk. A land dispute was also ongoing between the father and his elder son. The son then fired multiple bullets at his father, killing him instantly. Police have registered a murder case and are searching for the accused.

title: ICC reprimands Indian cricketer for throwing ball in 'dangerous manner' ahead of 2nd ODI
content: India fast bowler Gurnoor Brar was warned by the ICC and given one demerit point for breaching the Code of Conduct during the first ODI against England after throwing the ball towards Ben Duckett in a manner deemed "inappropriate and dangerous." Despite the incident, Brar took two wickets as India won by six wickets to lead the three-match series 1-0.

title: Another setback for TMC, Koel Mallick resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: TMC leader Koel Mallik resigned from the Rajya Sabha on Thursday. This follows Madan Mitra's departure and joining a rebel TMC faction. Earlier, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Sushmita Dev also resigned from the party. They subsequently joined the Bharatiya Janata Party and filed nomination papers. The BJP defended its decision to field these former TMC leaders.

title: Justin Gaethje's UFC future: Did his mother decide to end it?
content: Justin Gaethje’s future inside the UFC Octagon has become a hot topic after his stunning victory over Ilia Topuria at Freedom 250. The American shocked the MMA world by handing Topuria his first professional defeat and capturing the undisputed UFC lightweight title in the historic event held at the White House. While fans continue celebrating the memorable win, questions have already shifted toward whether Gaethje will fight again.

title: Messi magic and tactical panic: How England engineered their semi-final downfall
content: Thomas Tuchel’s plan worked until England scored, parked the bus and invited Lionel Messi to spend 37 minutes dismantling it. Read how England engineered its own downfall in today's Offside.

title: Unmanned wingmen: Drones fire air-to-air missiles, rewriting air combat rules
content: The US Air Force's YFQ-44A Fury drone successfully launched its first live AMRAAM missile. This historic test marks the first live missile launch by a US Collaborative Combat Aircraft. Anduril's Lattice software enabled autonomous mission execution and target engagement during the test. Other nations like Turkey and Australia are also advancing similar unmanned combat drone capabilities. These autonomous platforms are increasingly playing a central role in modern warfare scenarios.

title: Ukraine gets new PM: Parliament approves Serhii Koretskyi amid cabinet shuffle
content: Ukraine's parliament approved Serhii Koretskyi as the new prime minister on Thursday. This appointment follows the resignation of former prime minister Yulia Svyrydenko. President Zelenskyy cited Koretskyi's energy sector experience for winter preparations. The government reshuffle includes other senior officials and aims to refresh leadership. Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal as defense minister sparked protests and criticism.

title: Tiny creatures of sea expose big story of Gujarat’s plastic waste
content: Barnacles on Gujarat's coast show high microplastic contamination levels. Shivrajpur beach, a Blue Flag site, recorded the highest concentration of these particles. Researchers found microplastics in barnacles, seawater, and coastal sediments across thirteen locations. Plastic waste entering rivers and industrial effluents contribute significantly to this marine pollution. These findings highlight the widespread presence of plastic pollution in the marine ecosystem.

title: 'Diego is enjoying this immensely from up there': Messi's emotional tribute to Maradona
content: Argentina secured a place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after defeating England. Lionel Messi provided two assists, enabling Argentina to overcome a deficit. He dedicated the significant victory to the legendary Diego Maradona. This win mirrors Maradona's iconic performance against England forty years prior. Argentina will now face Spain in the championship match.

title: Army chief visits crucial Siliguri Corridor - why Chicken's Neck area is critical for India
content: India is actively enhancing infrastructure to secure its vital land bridge connecting the mainland to Northeast states. Railways plans a new underground line, and highway expansion is accelerating through central agencies. Land has been transferred to border security forces for improved surveillance and troop movement. The Army Chief reviewed security dynamics and modernization initiatives in the Eastern Command. He also outlined his vision for an agile and future-ready Indian Army.

title: 100% US tariff threat over Russian oil: India may have reasons to stay calm
content: This is not the first attempt by the US to penalise countries purchasing Russian oil. Last year, Washington imposed an additional 25% tariff on India over its imports of Russian crude, before reversing the measure in February.

title: Smith-Pooran showdown: Record breaking MLC game creates T20 history with ...
content: Nicholas Pooran, Andries Gous and Steven Smith all reached three figures in a game that rewrote multiple T20 records. While Pooran smashed a 31-ball hundred — the fastest century in Major League Cricket history — Gous and Smith responded with 40-ball hundreds each.

title: 'We will kill Trump': Tehran puts up gigantic billboard depicting US president in coffin
content: A large billboard showing Donald Trump in a coffin appeared in Tehran's Enghelab Square. This display coincided with renewed military strikes between the United States and Iran. US forces conducted strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. Iranian authorities reported a missile strike near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.

title: Messi, space & Argentina's relentless pressure: How final 10 mins broke England
content: Messi produced two assists in the last minutes as Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 on Wednesday (local time) and book a place in Sunday's World Cup final against Spain. Enzo Fernandez equalised in the 85th minute before Lautaro Martinez headed home Messi's cross in the second minute of stoppage time to complete another Argentina comeback.

title: Passport lost, stranded alone in Europe: Survival of Md Imran, India's upcoming GM
content: At 16, Indian chess prodigy Md Imran was stranded in Slovakia after a bus carrying his passport, visas and belongings left without him. Despite the ordeal, financial struggles and lack of institutional support, he played on to earn two Grandmaster norms. Now on the verge of becoming India's newest GM, Imran urgently awaits reissuance of his US visa to join his scholarship at UTRGV before August 23.

title: IRCTC's new website goes live: How it makes ticket booking easier for consumers
content: The Indian railway catering and tourism corporation has launched a beta version of its new website. This revamped portal offers a cleaner interface and a faster checkout process for passengers. Saved passenger details will enable quicker repeat bookings and a more convenient user experience. The ministry seeks user feedback to refine the platform before its final rollout. This improved system will soon integrate with the new Passenger Reservation Engine.

title: E20 fuel damages Maruti car; company ordered to give customer a new vehicle
content: A consumer court ordered Maruti Suzuki to replace a Grand Vitara with an E20-compatible model. The complainant alleged technical issues after E20 petrol became available. The court found the manufacturer and dealer guilty of deficiency in service. Maruti Suzuki must replace the SUV or pay its full value. The company also faces a compensation payment for mental agony.

title: 111 missiles, 0 hits: From Barak-8 to SM‑6 — Inside the layered defence of modern warships
content: Naval vessels face numerous threats, necessitating advanced defensive systems for protection. Modern warships employ missiles and guns to counter aerial and anti-ship threats. The United States uses maritime pressure as a lever against Iran today. Iranian missile salvos were likely intercepted by Standard-series missiles. These systems defend warships and merchant vessels from various attacks.

title: 'Stressful for students': SC tells Centre not to introduce 3rd language in Class 9
content: "No that is very bad. 9th standard is stressful. Why do you introduce a new language in 9th? You introduce it in 6th. When we were there in our school we had both ICSE and SSLC. We were taught both syllabus. Only at the end of 9th standard we had to select," Justice BV Nagarathna, who was hearing plea challenging Madras high court direction to establish Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas in each district, said according to Live Law.

title: TNLEA 2026 rank list released at tnlea.com: Direct link to download here
content: The Directorate of Technical Education has released the Tamil Nadu Lateral Entry Admissions 2026 rank list. Eligible candidates can now check their ranks by logging into the official website. Special counselling for TNLEA 2026 has also begun for eligible candidates. This counselling process facilitates direct admission to engineering programs for diploma holders. Candidates must complete all required steps within the given schedule to secure their seats.

title: India's first hydrogen train set for launch: All you need to know
content: India will soon launch its first hydrogen fuel cell train on Friday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will flag off the train in Haryana. This train generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen fuel. The project establishes India's first integrated hydrogen railway ecosystem. This initiative marks a significant step towards cleaner transport solutions.

title: 'Every life is precious': Delhi HC urges Centre to regularly examine Wangchuk's health
content: Hearing the a PIL over fasting activist's dwindling health, the high court noted that ​​"life is precious" and asked both the governments to provide, if needed, medical aid, to the Ladakh native. Doctors have warned that Wangchuk may soon enter a potentially "alarming" phase involving organ damage. Wangchuk has reportedly lost more than 9kgs since beginning his indefinite hunger strike.

title: Lessons from Iran war? Saudi Arabia to buy 20,000 laser guided rockets to counter drones
content: The United States has greenlit a major arms transaction with Saudi Arabia, encompassing the sale of twenty thousand precision-guided rockets focused on air defense. This acquisition aims to bolster Saudi Arabia's operational capacity against ground and aerial threats, responding to security concerns raised by recent regional conflicts. Furthermore, the sale equips Saudi forces to effectively counter the increasing challenges posed by drone warfare.

title: Karnataka govt proposes new apartment bill: Why it matters for flat owners
content: Karnataka plans a new law to govern apartment ownership and management. This legislation will replace outdated acts from nineteen seventy-two. The proposed bill clarifies land and common area ownership for apartment owners. It also introduces mandatory structural checks for older buildings. A two-tier dispute resolution system will be established under the new law.

title: Scientists reveal why killer whales struggle to hear blue whales
content: Some baleen whales use low-frequency songs to avoid killer whales. This acoustic crypsis helps flight species escape predators in open oceans. Blue whales, known for loud calls, sing at frequencies predators cannot hear. Natural selection favored these low-frequency calls over millions of years. This discovery reveals how predation shaped whale communication systems.

title: Woman Run Over By Speeding Dumper In Delhi, Driver Flees: Police
content: According to police, the impact of the accident was so severe that the victim's body was badly crushed and was initially difficult to identify.

title: New Zealand Issues Tsunami Warning After 6.3-Magnitude Earthquake
content: The epicenter of the earthquake was about 40 km north of Te Anau, which is the gateway to the tourist hotspot of Fiordland.

title: US Mint Begins Production Of Controversial $1 Coin Bearing Trump's Face
content: The coin is intended "to honor the enduring legacy of liberty and a lasting symbol of patriotism," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

title: What Takes PhDs Two Months, AI Is Doing In 2 Hours: Billionaire Investor
content: Citadel CEO Ken Griffin says their new agentic AI system can reproduce complex finance research in two to three hours instead of the six to eight weeks it takes master's and PhD-level employees.

title: US Considers Permanent Daylight Saving Time Again. How It Could Work
content: The House of Representatives on Tuesday voted to pass a bill to shift daylight saving time, when clocks are moved forward one hour, become permanent.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Settle Flat Amid Renewed US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Crude oil prices rise for 4th session amid escalating US-Iran war; Brent crude at $85.28.

title: "We Will Kill Trump": Iran's New Billboard Puts US President In A Coffin
content: The new hoarding shows the US president's body peeking out from an open black coffin. In the picture, his hair is disheveled, and his eyes and mouth are closed, as his hands rest on a red tie atop a protruding stomach.

title: Thane Woman "Honey-Traps" Ex-Army Officer, Extorts Rs 20 Lakh, Case Filed: Police
content: A doctor currently residing in Bhilwara, Rajasthan, alleged that the accused blackmailed him over two years between April 2024 and May 2026.

title: US Slaps Brazil With New 25% Tariff Over Unfair Trade Practices
content: The latest move also comes as President Donald Trump's administration pushes to rebuild his economic agenda after the US Supreme Court in February struck down a swath of his global tariffs.

title: Life At 48 Degrees Celsius: A Day In UP's Banda, One Of Hottest Towns On Earth
content: For many residents of Banda, a town that has recorded some of the country's highest temperatures, just getting through each day is a challenge.

title: PM Modi To Flag Off India's First Hydrogen Train In Haryana On Friday
content: The prime minister will lay the foundation stone and launch development projects worth around Rs 14,700 crore at Eklavya Stadium, Jind, on Friday.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Britain Is Finding It So Hard To Deport A Pakistani 'Grooming Gang' Leader
content: Shabir Ahmed, a UK-Pakistan dual national, lost his British citizenship after his conviction more than two decades ago. Now, Pakistan is refusing to take him back.

title: Kerala High Court Pauses Waqf Board Functioning Over Composition Row
content: A division bench comprising Chief Justice Soumen Sen and Justice V.M. Shyamkumar passed the interim order while hearing petitions filed by BJP leader Shaun George and the Assembly of Christian Trust Services (ACTS).

title: Gangster Nitish Kaushal, 26, Added To FBI's Wanted List
content: Gangster Nitish Kaushal, alias Lala, is accused of carrying out act of violence, including, but not limited to kidnappings and assaults.

title: "Allows To Operate At Absolute Best": US To Test Troops For Low Testosterone
content: The screenings will be conducted annually as part of service members' required medical screenings for those 30 and older.

title: US weighs $100,000 bond for some Green Card applicants abroad: What it means for Indians
